parameters.text:
=here is the output from dfir iris containing the alerts that were created:  {{ $json.combined }}

do the following:
- collect alert_id that relate to the same asset_name and suggest a case name based on the signatures in the alerts, these alerts will be merged later 
- extract the attacker IP form alert_description and target IP from alert_note
- count the number of akert_id

Return a JSON array similar to the following structure:


[
  {
    "case_name": "...",
    "alerts_to_merge": [..., ..., ...
    ],
    "attacker_ip": "...",
    "target_ip": "...",
    "alert_count": ...
  }
]

your answer shouldn't include any other working than the json array

options.systemMessage: You are a SOC automation agent using dfir IRIS

parameters.content: ## Create Case in IR Platform and Enrich it

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "alert_title": "{{ $ifEmpty($json.full_records.category, 'Unknown Category') }}",
  "alert_description": "{{ $ifEmpty($json.full_records.signature, 'Unknown Category') }} \n Attacker IP: {{ $json.full_records.flow.src_ip }}",
  "alert_source": "{{ $json.full_records.pkt_src }}",
  "alert_severity_id": 1,
  "alert_status_id": 1,
  "alert_customer_id": 1,
  "alert_note":"Community ID: {{ $json.full_records.community_id }} \nTarget IP: {{ $json.full_records.flow.dest_ip }}",
  "alert_source_event_time":"{{ $json.full_records['@timestamp'] }}",
  "alert_assets": [
    {
      "asset_ip": "{{ $json.full_records.flow.dest_ip }}",
      "asset_type_id": 3,
      "asset_name": "{{ $json.full_records.dest_ip }}",
      "asset_ip": "{{ $json.full_records.dest_ip }}"
    }
  ]
}
